name: Rupa Gosvami
dates_notes: Mughal court records (former finance minister); dates well-established.

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court records of his pre-conversion administrative role.
Manuscript colophons across the Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu and other works.

core_thesis:
Rūpa Gosvāmī's contribution is to convert Caitanya's experiential teaching into a systematic theology of *bhakti-rasa* that is simultaneously Sanskrit aesthetics and metaphysics. The conversion is precise rather than ornamental, and it does specific argumentative work for the *acintya-bhedābheda* program.

The definition of *bhakti* is given at [*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 1.1.11](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/1.1.11): *anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyaṃ jñāna-karmādy-anāvṛtam ānukūlyena kṛṣṇānuśīlanaṃ bhaktir uttamā* — devotion uncontaminated by any other desire and unobstructed by the alternative *puruṣārthas* of *karma* and *jñāna*, the favorable cultivation of relation to Kṛṣṇa. Each qualifier marks a structural exclusion. *Anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyam* excludes desire for any object other than Kṛṣṇa. *Jñāna-karmādy-anāvṛtam* excludes the construal of *bhakti* as a means whose end lies in *jñāna* or *karma*; *bhakti* is its own *prayojana*.

The second move is the extension of classical *rasa*-theory. Bharata's *Nāṭya-Śāstra* and Abhinavagupta's *Abhinava-Bhāratī* establish a finite typology of aesthetic *rasas* with corresponding *sthāyi-bhāvas*. Rūpa argues that *bhakti* is itself a *rasa* — *bhakti-rasa* — with *prema* as its *sthāyi-bhāva* and Bhagavān as its *viṣaya-ālambana*. Five primary *rasas* are graded by intimacy: *śānta* (peaceful contemplation), *dāsya* (servant), *sakhya* (friend), *vātsalya* (parental), *mādhurya* (conjugal). The grading is not a hierarchy of esteem but a structural ordering of how the *jīva* relates to Kṛṣṇa under increasingly intimate *vibhāva*-conditions.

The ontological precondition is that the *jīva* is *taṭastha-śakti* of Bhagavān, eternally distinct and eternally non-different, constitutively constituted for relational *prema*. *Sādhana-bhakti* in its two modes — *vaidhī* (rule-governed) and *rāgānugā* (following spontaneous love of the eternal Vraja-residents) — matures through *bhāva* into *prema*. The goal is participation in Kṛṣṇa-*līlā* in one's *siddha-deha*, never absorption. Rūpa's framework is the doctrinal interface through which Caitanya's *acintya-bhedābheda* enters Sanskrit philosophical and aesthetic discourse and acquires the technical vocabulary by which it can be defended in those discourses' terms.

engaged_works.title: Bhakti-Rasamrta-Sindhu
engaged_works.summary: The *Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* is the Gauḍīya school's foundational systematic treatise on *bhakti-rasa*. Four *vibhāgas* organize it: *pūrva* on the general nature of *bhakti* (the definition at 1.1.11, *anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyam*); *dakṣiṇa* on *sādhana-bhakti* in its *vaidhī* and *rāgānugā* modes ([*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 1.2](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/1.2) and 1.2.270 ff. respectively); *paścima* on *bhāva-bhakti* ([*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 1.3](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/1.3)); *uttara* on *prema-bhakti* and the analysis of the five primary *rasas* ([*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 2–4](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/2-4), with the rasa-typology at 2.5). The structural move that organizes the whole is the transposition of the classical Sanskrit *rasa-sūtra* — Bharata (*Nāṭya-Śāstra* 6.31, *vibhāvānubhāva-vyabhicāri-saṃyogāt rasa-niṣpattiḥ*), Abhinavagupta (*Abhinava-Bhāratī* on *Nāṭya-Śāstra* 6), Mammaṭa (*Kāvya-Prakāśa* 4) — into a theological register. *Vibhāva*, *anubhāva*, *sāttvika-bhāva*, and *vyabhicāri-bhāva* combine in the qualified *bhakta* to generate the *sthāyi-bhāva* of devotion, by which Bhagavān is relished as *rasa* (*āsvāda*) ([*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 2.1.5](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/2.1.5), glossing *rati* as the *sthāyī* of *bhakti-rasa*). The transposition is not metaphor: Rūpa treats the *rasa*-categories as functioning the same way they do in poetics, but with Bhagavān as the proper *ālambana* ([*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 2.1.16](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/2.1.16), *kṛṣṇaś ca tad-bhaktāś ca viṣayāśrayau*). The work serves three functions at once. It is a devotional manual, organizing practice. It is a technical treatise within Sanskrit aesthetic theory, defensible to a *Naiyāyika* or an *Ālaṅkārika* on their own terms. And it is metaphysical exposition: the *jīva*'s status as eternal *taṭastha-śakti* is the precondition for *bhakti-rasa* being a real, eternal participation rather than a transient mood (the structural point given in the *kāraṇa-vibhāga* of [*Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* 2.1](cite://rupa-gosvami/bhakti-rasamrta-sindhu/2.1)). The text becomes the principal Gauḍīya reference for all subsequent *rasa*-theological work and the structural basis for both Jīva's *Prīti-Sandarbha* and Viśvanātha Cakravartī's manuals.

engaged_works.title: Ujjvala-Nilamani
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored extension of Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu on madhura-rasa.
engaged_works.summary: The *Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* extends the *uttara-vibhāga* of the *Bhakti-Rasāmṛta-Sindhu* into a dedicated treatise on *madhura-rasa* — the erotic-devotional mode in which Kṛṣṇa is related to as *priyatama* and the paradigmatic *bhaktas* are the *gopīs* of Vraja, with Rādhā as their crown ([*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 1](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/1), *kṛṣṇa-prakaraṇa*; 4, *nāyikā-prakaraṇa*). The taxonomy is granular and technical. Classifications of *nāyikā*- and *nāyaka*-types are adapted from classical *kāvya* poetics ([*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 4–5](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/4-5), against the typology of Bharata's *Nāṭya-Śāstra* 24 and Mammaṭa's *Kāvya-Prakāśa*). The ten stages of love are mapped from *prema* through *snēha*, *māna*, *praṇaya*, *rāga*, *anurāga*, and *bhāva* to *mahābhāva* ([*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 14](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/14), *sthāyi-bhāva-prakaraṇa* — the *prema-vaicittya* progression). The conditions and *anubhāvas* of *vipralambha* (love-in-separation) and *sambhoga* (union) are distinguished ([*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 15](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/15), *vipralambha-prakaraṇa*; 15.105 ff. on *sambhoga*). *Adhirūḍha-mahābhāva* — the highest intensification of *mahābhāva* — is treated as occurring in Rādhā alone ([*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 14.156–168](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/14.156-168)). The metaphysical load of the analysis is substantial. *Mādhurya-rasa* is presented not as one option among several but as the most complete actualisation of *acintya-bhedābheda*: the mode in which the *jīva*'s real distinctness from Bhagavān (as *priyā* to *priyatama*) and real non-difference (as *śakti* to *śaktimat*) are simultaneously at their most acute (the structural argument at [*Ujjvala-Nīlamaṇi* 1.21](cite://rupa-gosvami/ujjvala-nilamani/1.21) ff. on Rādhā as *hlādinī-śakti-svarūpa*). The work is the canonical Gauḍīya reference for Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa rasa-theology and the structural template for all subsequent treatments.

engaged_works.title: Laghu-Bhagavatamrta
engaged_works.summary: The *Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* is Rūpa's compact but doctrinally dense compendium of *bhāgavata-tattva*. Two parts organize it. *Kṛṣṇa-amṛta* treats the nature of Bhagavān, his *avatāras*, and the doctrine of Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* (*Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta*, *pūrva-khaṇḍa* / *kṛṣṇa-amṛta*). *Bhakta-amṛta* treats the typology of devotees and the structure of *bhakti* (*Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta*, *uttara-khaṇḍa* / *bhakta-amṛta*). The constructive achievement is the canonical hierarchy of divine manifestation: *svayaṃ-rūpa* (Kṛṣṇa as Kṛṣṇa), *tad-ekātma-rūpa* (forms of one essence with him), and *āveśa-rūpa* (forms in which he is empowered to enter) ([*Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 1.1.12](cite://rupa-gosvami/laghu-bhagavatamrta/1.1.12) ff., the threefold *prakāśa*-typology). The school-defining thesis is defended by anchored *Bhāgavata* citation — most consequentially *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28, *kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam* (the verse argued at [*Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* 1.5](cite://rupa-gosvami/laghu-bhagavatamrta/1.5)) — that Kṛṣṇa is not an *avatāra* of Viṣṇu but *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, the source of all other manifestations including Viṣṇu. The typology of *bhakti*-stages and devotee-classes set out in the second part is the structural source for Jīva's later expansion in the *Bhagavat*-, *Kṛṣṇa*-, and *Bhakti-Sandarbhas* (most directly at *Kṛṣṇa-Sandarbha* §28–29). Concise enough to be memorized and exegetically dense enough to license sub-commentary, the *Laghu-Bhāgavatāmṛta* functions as the school's portable doctrinal map of Bhagavān and his *bhaktas*.
